ground_truth.description: A code change in search-service v6.3.0 introduced a memory leak in the query cache. Memory usage grew gradually over 2 hours until OOM kills triggered. The only recent deployment was to an unrelated service (recommendation-service) 14 hours earlier.
key_evidence: search-service memory usage grew linearly from 60% to 95% over 2 hours
OOM kills with sawtooth memory pattern
No deployments to search-service in last 24 hours

metrics.query: latency and error rate for all services, last 3 hours

search-service.note: Memory grows linearly then OOM kill, restart at ~60%, grows again. Classic memory leak sawtooth.

recommendation-service.note: recommendation-service is completely stable. Its recent deployment is unrelated.

alerts.query: all alerts in the last 3 hours

alerts.message: search-service memory utilization exceeded 90% with linear growth pattern over 2 hours
search-service pod OOMKilled — sawtooth restart pattern: crash, restart, memory grows, crash again
search-service GC overhead >95% — spending almost all CPU time in garbage collection

deployments.query: all deployments in the last 24 hours

deployments.change_summary: Updated recommendation ML model weights to v2.1 — model-only update, no code changes

response.note: No deployments to search-service in the last 24 hours. Last search-service deploy (v6.3.0) was 3 days ago on 2024-11-19.

log_analysis.query: error log patterns for all services, last 3 hours

response.analysis_summary: Classic memory leak detected in search-service. The QueryCacheManager.put() method is the top memory allocator, growing unboundedly without eviction. Sawtooth crash-restart-grow-crash pattern confirms a leak rather than a load spike. No correlation with any recent deployment — the only recent deploy was to recommendation-service which is healthy.

patterns.message_template: GC overhead limit exceeded: 98% time in GC, freed <2% heap. Top allocator: QueryCacheManager (4.2GB/4.5GB heap)

response.current_error_rate: ~1800 errors/min during OOM, drops after restart, climbs again

alerts_misleading.query: all alerts in the last 3 hours

alerts.message: search-service p99 latency exceeded 5000ms after rollback deployment
recommendation-service model inference latency elevated — possible model issue after v2.1 update

deployments_misleading.query: all deployments in the last 24 hours

deployments.change_summary: HOTFIX ATTEMPT: Increased heap size to 8GB — deployment failed during rolling update
Updated recommendation ML model weights to v2.1 — includes new embedding dimensions

response.note: Multiple recent deploys visible: a failed hotfix to search-service, a rollback, and a recommendation-service model update from last night.

logs_misleading.query: error log patterns for all services, last 3 hours

response.analysis_summary: search-service showing instability after rollback deployment. Rollback may have introduced configuration inconsistencies. Additionally, recommendation-service showing elevated model inference times since v2.1 model update.

patterns.message_template: Warning: search-service config version mismatch detected after rollback — cache settings from v6.3.0 persisted in shared state
Model inference slow: v2.1 model taking 3x longer than v2.0.9 on embedding computation

response.current_error_rate: ~380 errors/min, mixed errors across search and recommendation services

alerts_post_resolution.query: all alerts in the last 3 hours

deployments_post_rollback.query: all deployments in the last 24 hours

deployments.change_summary: ROLLBACK: Reverted search-service to v6.2.9 to fix memory leak in QueryCacheManager
HOTFIX ATTEMPT: Increased heap size to 8GB — did not resolve, memory still growing
Updated recommendation ML model weights to v2.1

logs_pre_incident.query: error log patterns for all services, last 3 hours

response.analysis_summary: Normal operations. No anomalies detected in any service.

log_analysis_different_reasoning.query: error log patterns for all services, last 3 hours

response.analysis_summary: Infrastructure capacity issue detected. The search-service JVM is running out of heap space under current query load. This appears to be a capacity planning issue — the service needs more memory to handle the current traffic volume, not a code defect.

patterns.message_template: JVM heap exhausted under load — application requires more memory for current query volume
patterns.suggested_cause: Insufficient heap allocation. Recommend scaling vertically (increase heap) or horizontally (add instances).

patterns.message_template: Cache utilization at 100%, eviction rate cannot keep pace with growth
patterns.suggested_cause: Query cache sized too small for current traffic. Increase cache max size or add LRU eviction.

response.current_error_rate: ~1800 errors/min, categorized as infrastructure capacity issues

alerts_new_schema.query: all alerts in the last 3 hours
